short_desc: The babak, including its glacier variant the yeti, is a type of lurker in The Precursor Legacy, Daxter, and Jak II. They are introduced as enemies, being the most common foot soldiers in Gol Acheron and Maia's army. They eventually evolved and became non-hostile, and were the species of the character Brutter and his tribe of lurker people, domesticated and enslaved by Haven citizens.
full_text:
The babak, including its glacier variant the yeti, is a type of lurker in The Precursor Legacy, Daxter, and Jak II. They are introduced as enemies, being the most common foot soldiers in Gol Acheron and Maia's army. They eventually evolved and became non-hostile, and were the species of the character Brutter and his tribe of lurker people, domesticated and enslaved by Haven citizens.

The babak is perhaps the most representative and common lurker. It is some species of primate, with magenta fur, a large mane, and physical characteristics common to all lurkers, including skin-colored spurs along its jawline, and bright yellow eyes. Babaks are simple one-hit enemies and are commonly encountered in groups.

Babaks represent one of five known non-human sapient species in the Jak and Daxter series, accompanied by ottsels, Kor's species, Kaeden's species, and moncaws. However, only Brutter was ever shown to speak. They are also among lurker sharks and jungle fish as the only lurkers known to have survived the 300 years between The Precursor Legacy and Jak II.
paragraphs: The babak, including its glacier variant the yeti, is a type of lurker in The Precursor Legacy, Daxter, and Jak II. They are introduced as enemies, being the most common foot soldiers in Gol Acheron and Maia's army. They eventually evolved and became non-hostile, and were the species of the character Brutter and his tribe of lurker people, domesticated and enslaved by Haven citizens.

The babak is perhaps the most representative and common lurker. It is some species of primate, with magenta fur, a large mane, and physical characteristics common to all lurkers, including skin-colored spurs along its jawline, and bright yellow eyes. Babaks are simple one-hit enemies and are commonly encountered in groups.

Babaks represent one of five known non-human sapient species in the Jak and Daxter series, accompanied by ottsels, Kor's species, Kaeden's species, and moncaws. However, only Brutter was ever shown to speak. They are also among lurker sharks and jungle fish as the only lurkers known to have survived the 300 years between The Precursor Legacy and Jak II.

short_desc: The bone armor lurker is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a lurker only seen on Misty Island. It was a bone armor lurker that confronted Jak and Daxter in the opening cutscene of the game, causing Daxter's transformation into an ottsel.
full_text: The bone armor lurker is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a lurker only seen on Misty Island. It was a bone armor lurker that confronted Jak and Daxter in the opening cutscene of the game, causing Daxter's transformation into an ottsel.
paragraphs: The bone armor lurker is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a lurker only seen on Misty Island. It was a bone armor lurker that confronted Jak and Daxter in the opening cutscene of the game, causing Daxter's transformation into an ottsel.

short_desc: The quicksand lurker or plunger lurker (depending on the context), also known as the fireball lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a small, purple lurker indigenous to the mud ponds of Misty Island.
full_text:
The quicksand lurker or plunger lurker (depending on the context), also known as the fireball lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a small, purple lurker indigenous to the mud ponds of Misty Island.

They act primarily as sentries, guarding areas of the island from intruders such as Jak and Daxter. One ventured out as far as Mountain Pass, though they were never seen outside of their pond while on Misty Island.
paragraphs: The quicksand lurker or plunger lurker (depending on the context), also known as the fireball lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a small, purple lurker indigenous to the mud ponds of Misty Island.

They act primarily as sentries, guarding areas of the island from intruders such as Jak and Daxter. One ventured out as far as Mountain Pass, though they were never seen outside of their pond while on Misty Island.

short_desc: The mine dropper, also known as the lurker balloon, is a vehicle operated by a balloon lurker, also known as a mine-dropping lurker, in The Precursor Legacy. Several of these were seen flying around the bay near the Lurker ship at Misty Island.
full_text: The mine dropper, also known as the lurker balloon, is a vehicle operated by a balloon lurker, also known as a mine-dropping lurker, in The Precursor Legacy. Several of these were seen flying around the bay near the Lurker ship at Misty Island.
paragraphs: The mine dropper, also known as the lurker balloon, is a vehicle operated by a balloon lurker, also known as a mine-dropping lurker, in The Precursor Legacy. Several of these were seen flying around the bay near the Lurker ship at Misty Island.

short_desc: The lurker puppy is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a small lurker, patrolling the higher regions of Sentinel Beach. They are a maroon color, bearing close resemblance to a small dog. They obtain typical lurker traits such as appendages along their jawline, large yellow eyes, and a metal collar. They bark upon sighting an enemy, and make a whimpering sound when they are hit, similar to canines.
full_text:
The lurker puppy is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a small lurker, patrolling the higher regions of Sentinel Beach. They are a maroon color, bearing close resemblance to a small dog. They obtain typical lurker traits such as appendages along their jawline, large yellow eyes, and a metal collar. They bark upon sighting an enemy, and make a whimpering sound when they are hit, similar to canines.

thumb|left|Concept art.
They are one of the weakest type of lurker, taking only one hit before they die. Any melee attack will suffice for killing them. They are very easy to outrun or sidestep.
paragraphs: The lurker puppy is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a small lurker, patrolling the higher regions of Sentinel Beach. thumb|left|Concept art.
They are one of the weakest type of lurker, taking only one hit before they die. Any melee attack will suffice for killing them. They are very easy to outrun or sidestep.

short_desc: The lurker crab is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy found at Sentinel Beach. They closely resemble hermit crabs as they hide under their shell until they reach out for an attack. Lurker crabs, similar to hermit crabs, have a large conch-like shell which they carry on top of their red bodies. They have beady crazed eyes with large pincers, and, despite their size, still carry the typical lurker trait of overgrowing appendages over their jawline.
full_text:
The lurker crab is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy found at Sentinel Beach. They closely resemble hermit crabs as they hide under their shell until they reach out for an attack. Lurker crabs, similar to hermit crabs, have a large conch-like shell which they carry on top of their red bodies. They have beady crazed eyes with large pincers, and, despite their size, still carry the typical lurker trait of overgrowing appendages over their jawline.

thumb|left|Concept art
A lurker crab will hide under its shell until it gets close enough to an enemy to reach out and pinch it. Other than that it has no special attacks distinguishing it from a normal crab. Wait for the crab to reveal itself before hitting it—otherwise it will just spin away taking no damage. This does not go for the cannon, as its explosives will blow the crab away as long as it is close enough.
paragraphs: The lurker crab is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy found at Sentinel Beach. They closely resemble hermit crabs as they hide under their shell until they reach out for an attack. This does not go for the cannon, as its explosives will blow the crab away as long as it is close enough.

short_desc: The sand worm, also known as the sea serpent lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They are lurkers dwelling in sand pits at Sentinel Beach.
full_text: The sand worm, also known as the sea serpent lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They are lurkers dwelling in sand pits at Sentinel Beach.
paragraphs: The sand worm, also known as the sea serpent lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They are lurkers dwelling in sand pits at Sentinel Beach.

short_desc:
* Hopper (lurker), a type of lurker encountered in the Forbidden Jungle during The Precursor Legacy.
* Hopper (metal head), a type of metal head encountered at Mountain Temple and Haven City during Jak II.
* Hopper bot, a type of death bot encountered in the Haven sewers during Jak 3.
full_text:
* Hopper (lurker), a type of lurker encountered in the Forbidden Jungle during The Precursor Legacy.
* Hopper (metal head), a type of metal head encountered at Mountain Temple and Haven City during Jak II.
* Hopper bot, a type of death bot encountered in the Haven sewers during Jak 3.
paragraphs:
* Hopper (lurker), a type of lurker encountered in the Forbidden Jungle during The Precursor Legacy.
* Hopper (metal head), a type of metal head encountered at Mountain Temple and Haven City during Jak II.
* Hopper bot, a type of death bot encountered in the Haven sewers during Jak 3.

short_desc: The lurker snake, also known as the jungle snake or lurker python, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They are large, brown serpentine lurkers residing in the canopy of Forbidden Jungle. They primarily act as sentries, hanging from branches at fixed points in the level to attack intruders, such as Jak.
full_text: The lurker snake, also known as the jungle snake or lurker python, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They are large, brown serpentine lurkers residing in the canopy of Forbidden Jungle. They primarily act as sentries, hanging from branches at fixed points in the level to attack intruders, such as Jak.

They primarily act as sentries, hanging from branches at fixed points in the level to attack intruders, such as Jak.

short_desc: The jungle fish, also known as the fish lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy and a background creature in Jak X: Combat Racing. They are some species of fish that were transformed into lurkers by Maia through dark eco sorcery, and are among babaks and lurker sharks as the only lurkers known to have survived in the time between The Precursor Legacy and Jak II.
full_text: The jungle fish, also known as the fish lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy and a background creature in Jak X: Combat Racing. They are some species of fish that were transformed into lurkers by Maia through dark eco sorcery, and are among babaks and lurker sharks as the only lurkers known to have survived in the time between The Precursor Legacy and Jak II.
paragraphs: The jungle fish, also known as the fish lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy and a background creature in Jak X: Combat Racing. They are some species of fish that were transformed into lurkers by Maia through dark eco sorcery, and are among babaks and lurker sharks as the only lurkers known to have survived in the time between The Precursor Legacy and Jak II.

short_desc: The aphid lurker is an insectoid lurker enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They spawn from the dark eco plant to defend it during the mission "Defeat the dark eco plant" within the Forbidden Temple. The creature is a stubby, green, spiked insect featuring typical lurker traits, including large yellow eyes, jawline appendages, and a solid necklace.
full_text:
The aphid lurker is an insectoid lurker enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They spawn from the dark eco plant to defend it during the mission "Defeat the dark eco plant" within the Forbidden Temple. The creature is a stubby, green, spiked insect featuring typical lurker traits, including large yellow eyes, jawline appendages, and a solid necklace.

thumb|left|The aphid lurker without its spikes protruding.
Combat with the aphid lurker consists of two phases: an offensive stage and a vulnerable stage, similar to the ice lurker and the puffer. In its offensive stage, spikes protrude across its body, making it impossible to attack without taking damage. In its vulnerable stage, it retracts these spikes, leaving it open to attack.
paragraphs: The aphid lurker is an insectoid lurker enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They spawn from the dark eco plant to defend it during the mission "Defeat the dark eco plant" within the Forbidden Temple. The creature is a stubby, green, spiked insect featuring typical lurker traits, including large yellow eyes, jawline appendages, and a solid necklace.

In its offensive stage, spikes protrude across its body, making it impossible to attack without taking damage. In its vulnerable stage, it retracts these spikes, leaving it open to attack.

short_desc: The dark eco plant is a boss-level enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a massively mutated dark eco plant located in the Forbidden Temple. The dark eco plant is the first boss in The Precursor Legacy, although its defeat is entirely optional. Living in the Forbidden Jungle's temple, the plant's roots have gradually buried into a pool of dark eco below, severely mutating it. The plant's vines extend into the Jungle outside and serve as minor but hazardous obstacles.
full_text: The dark eco plant is a boss-level enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a massively mutated dark eco plant located in the Forbidden Temple. The dark eco plant is the first boss in The Precursor Legacy, although its defeat is entirely optional. Living in the Forbidden Jungle's temple, the plant's roots have gradually buried into a pool of dark eco below, severely mutating it. The plant's vines extend into the Jungle outside and serve as minor but hazardous obstacles.

The plant's vines extend into the Jungle outside and serve as minor but hazardous obstacles.

short_desc: The double lurker is a team of enemies in The Precursor Legacy. There are two types of blue-tinted lurkers which comprise the duo lurkers, the two of which have very similar traits, though one being smaller than the other, and—as a consequence—pair up together with one on top of the other to provide a larger, stronger enemy.
full_text:
The double lurker is a team of enemies in The Precursor Legacy. There are two types of blue-tinted lurkers which comprise the duo lurkers, the two of which have very similar traits, though one being smaller than the other, and—as a consequence—pair up together with one on top of the other to provide a larger, stronger enemy.

Two blue lurkers, one of them being larger and stronger with a plain face, and the other being smaller and appears to be somewhat crazed. They bear similar lurker traits such as large yellow eyes, metallic collars, and appendages growing over their jawline.

thumb|left|A separated double lurker team.
The bottom lurker cannot be defeated unless the top lurker is knocked off his shoulders. This can be done by jumping and kicking (+). Once the two are separated each can be destroyed with one melee attack.
paragraphs: The double lurker is a team of enemies in The Precursor Legacy. There are two types of blue-tinted lurkers which comprise the duo lurkers, the two of which have very similar traits, though one being smaller than the other, and—as a consequence—pair up together with one on top of the other to provide a larger, stronger enemy.

Two blue lurkers, one of them being larger and stronger with a plain face, and the other being smaller and appears to be somewhat crazed. They bear similar lurker traits such as large yellow eyes, metallic collars, and appendages growing over their jawline.

thumb|left|A separated double lurker team.
The bottom lurker cannot be defeated unless the top lurker is knocked off his shoulders. This can be done by jumping and kicking (+). Once the two are separated each can be destroyed with one melee attack.

short_desc: The puffer, also known as a flying lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy, found in lost Precursor city.
full_text:
The puffer, also known as a flying lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy, found in lost Precursor city.

Puffers are large, purple lurkers that fly around in circles with bat-like wings similar to the robber. The lighting of lost Precursor city is intense to the point of making the puffers (and other objects and creatures) appear blue. Their physical characteristic traits are common to all lurkers, including appendages growing over their jawline and large yellow eyes.

thumb|left|The puffer in its distended state.
Similar to the aphid lurker and ice lurker, the puffer has two stages of combat. The first stage is offensive, inflating themselves with spikes (similar to a blowfish). During the second stage, they are vulnerable to attack when they retract their spikes.
paragraphs: The puffer, also known as a flying lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy, found in lost Precursor city.

Puffers are large, purple lurkers that fly around in circles with bat-like wings similar to the robber. The lighting of lost Precursor city is intense to the point of making the puffers (and other objects and creatures) appear blue. Their physical characteristic traits are common to all lurkers, including appendages growing over their jawline and large yellow eyes.

thumb|left|The puffer in its distended state.
Similar to the aphid lurker and ice lurker, the puffer has two stages of combat. The first stage is offensive, inflating themselves with spikes (similar to a blowfish). During the second stage, they are vulnerable to attack when they retract their spikes.

short_desc: The bully, also known as the spinning lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They were only found in pool hubs at the lost Precursor city.
full_text:
The bully, also known as the spinning lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They were only found in pool hubs at the lost Precursor city.

They appear to be very similar to bone armor lurkers, having blue skin and bone armor, but are much more shorter and stouter. They are enclosed in a wooden cage that is spiked, which they use to inflict harm on intruders by spinning around in circles.

The only way to destroy these lurkers is to jump and dive (+), attacking their vulnerable spot on top of their cage, not supported by any formidable structure or defensive spikes. It is easier to destroy these lurkers when they are stationary, as they pause for a moment out of dizziness after spinning for about ten seconds. Finding the opportunity to strike can be difficult, however, due to them always being grouped together.
paragraphs: The bully, also known as the spinning lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They were only found in pool hubs at the lost Precursor city.

They appear to be very similar to bone armor lurkers, having blue skin and bone armor, but are much more shorter and stouter. They are enclosed in a wooden cage that is spiked, which they use to inflict harm on intruders by spinning around in circles.

The only way to destroy these lurkers is to jump and dive (+), attacking their vulnerable spot on top of their cage, not supported by any formidable structure or defensive spikes. It is easier to destroy these lurkers when they are stationary, as they pause for a moment out of dizziness after spinning for about ten seconds. Finding the opportunity to strike can be difficult, however, due to them always being grouped together.

short_desc: The swamp rat, also known as the rat lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They appeared to be mutated rats while bearing lurker traits, dwelling in the poisonous mud of Boggy Swamp. They spawn from nests which have the appearance of small trees.
full_text:
The swamp rat, also known as the rat lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They appeared to be mutated rats while bearing lurker traits, dwelling in the poisonous mud of Boggy Swamp. They spawn from nests which have the appearance of small trees.

thumb|left|A swamp rat nest
They are small, red-orange rats having beady yellow eyes and appendages growing over their jawline typical to all lurkers. They also bear metal collars which all lurkers, with the exception of a few, wear.

thumb|Jak charging a group of swamp rats with the [[flut flut.]]
These lurkers are incredibly weak, possibly the weakest, and are not hard to evade. However, they spawn in sometimes incredibly large groups, and having multitudes of these swamp rats attack, with the addition to other hazards such as dark eco boxes, swamp thorns, and other lurkers, can be a nuisance. During the mission "Protect Farthy's snacks", Jak must defeat these enemies by channeling yellow eco excessively in order to defend Farthy's snacks in hopes of it coming back to its owner, Boggy Billy.
paragraphs: The swamp rat, also known as the rat lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They appeared to be mutated rats while bearing lurker traits, dwelling in the poisonous mud of Boggy Swamp. They spawn from nests which have the appearance of small trees.

thumb|left|A swamp rat nest
They are small, red-orange rats having beady yellow eyes and appendages growing over their jawline typical to all lurkers. They also bear metal collars which all lurkers, with the exception of a few, wear.

thumb|Jak charging a group of swamp rats with the [[flut flut.]]
These lurkers are incredibly weak, possibly the weakest, and are not hard to evade. However, they spawn in sometimes incredibly large groups, and having multitudes of these swamp rats attack, with the addition to other hazards such as dark eco boxes, swamp thorns, and other lurkers, can be a nuisance. During the mission "Protect Farthy's snacks", Jak must defeat these enemies by channeling yellow eco excessively in order to defend Farthy's snacks in hopes of it coming back to its owner, Boggy Billy.

short_desc: The swamp bat is an enemy during The Precursor Legacy, commonly found flying in place in swarms near Boggy Swamp.
full_text:
The swamp bat is an enemy during The Precursor Legacy, commonly found flying in place in swarms near Boggy Swamp.

Swamp bats are large flying creatures with typical lurker features such as appendages over their jawline and large yellow eyes. They are a red color, making them easy to spot in contrast with the swamp's dark green appearance.

Swamp bats fly in swarms of six other bats, swooping in one at a time towards their target upon approach, biting them. It is simple to side step their course, and can be defeated by using yellow eco to jump and shoot them during their flight. They can also be destroyed using basic melee attacks as they arrive one-by-one.
paragraphs: The swamp bat is an enemy during The Precursor Legacy, commonly found flying in place in swarms near Boggy Swamp.

Swamp bats are large flying creatures with typical lurker features such as appendages over their jawline and large yellow eyes. They are a red color, making them easy to spot in contrast with the swamp's dark green appearance.

Swamp bats fly in swarms of six other bats, swooping in one at a time towards their target upon approach, biting them. It is simple to side step their course, and can be defeated by using yellow eco to jump and shoot them during their flight. They can also be destroyed using basic melee attacks as they arrive one-by-one.

short_desc: The lurker toad is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a large, purple frog lurker found hopping around aimlessly in Boggy Swamp, making a very loud croaking noise. They bear typical lurker traits such as appendages along their jaw line, and large, yellow eyes.
full_text:
The lurker toad is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a large, purple frog lurker found hopping around aimlessly in Boggy Swamp, making a very loud croaking noise. They bear typical lurker traits such as appendages along their jaw line, and large, yellow eyes.

thumb|left|Jak grappled by a lurker toad.
In combat it will hop towards Jak until he is in range, where it then launches its sticky tongue at him. If it connects it will stay stuck and the frog will slowly charge electricity through its tongue while also pulling Jak in. The only way to escape is to forcefully pull and punch away from the frog. To defeat it; a roll jump is suggested to get in close and then quickly take it out with a punch or kick. Its size and nature makes it a rather slow enemy.
paragraphs: The lurker toad is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. It is a large, purple frog lurker found hopping around aimlessly in Boggy Swamp, making a very loud croaking noise. They bear typical lurker traits such as appendages along their jaw line, and large, yellow eyes.

thumb|left|Jak grappled by a lurker toad.
In combat it will hop towards Jak until he is in range, where it then launches its sticky tongue at him. If it connects it will stay stuck and the frog will slowly charge electricity through its tongue while also pulling Jak in. The only way to escape is to forcefully pull and punch away from the frog. To defeat it; a roll jump is suggested to get in close and then quickly take it out with a punch or kick. Its size and nature makes it a rather slow enemy.

short_desc: The driller lurker is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy that mines cave rock away from the Precursor robot in Spider Cave with its drill.
full_text: The driller lurker is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy that mines cave rock away from the Precursor robot in Spider Cave with its drill.
paragraphs: The driller lurker is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy that mines cave rock away from the Precursor robot in Spider Cave with its drill.

short_desc: The gnawing lurker is a species of lurker encountered at Spider Cave during The Precursor Legacy. They are found throughout the cave gnawing on wooden support beams. Jak and Daxter are tasked with eliminating them in the mission "Use your goggles to shoot the gnawing lurkers", after which they receive a power cell.
full_text: The gnawing lurker is a species of lurker encountered at Spider Cave during The Precursor Legacy. They are found throughout the cave gnawing on wooden support beams. Jak and Daxter are tasked with eliminating them in the mission "Use your goggles to shoot the gnawing lurkers", after which they receive a power cell.
paragraphs: The gnawing lurker is a species of lurker encountered at Spider Cave during The Precursor Legacy. They are found throughout the cave gnawing on wooden support beams. Jak and Daxter are tasked with eliminating them in the mission "Use your goggles to shoot the gnawing lurkers", after which they receive a power cell.

short_desc: The citadel bunny and snow bunny are enemies in The Precursor Legacy, encountered at Gol and Maia's citadel and Snowy Mountain, respectively.
full_text:
The citadel bunny and snow bunny are enemies in The Precursor Legacy, encountered at Gol and Maia's citadel and Snowy Mountain, respectively.

The citadel bunny possesses purple-blue fur, small horns, and disproportionately large, powerful feet relative to its small, vestigial arms. These creatures are mass-produced by three machines within the citadel, which can only be deactivated by destroying their corresponding generators. Jak can utilize nearby red and yellow eco vents to clear a path through the swarms.

The snow bunny features light-blue skin, providing natural camouflage within snowy environments. These creatures possess large blue legs and tiny horns, and they exhibit rabbit-like hopping movements. While they can be defeated using any standard attack, Jak can prevent them from jumping over him by channeling red eco. Jak fought through a large number of these enemies during the "Survive the lurker-infested cave" mission.

Gol and Maia's citadel 3.png|Citadel bunnies atop their generators in the citadel.
paragraphs: The citadel bunny and snow bunny are enemies in The Precursor Legacy, encountered at Gol and Maia's citadel and Snowy Mountain, respectively.

The citadel bunny possesses purple-blue fur, small horns, and disproportionately large, powerful feet relative to its small, vestigial arms. These creatures are mass-produced by three machines within the citadel, which can only be deactivated by destroying their corresponding generators. Jak can utilize nearby red and yellow eco vents to clear a path through the swarms.

The snow bunny features light-blue skin, providing natural camouflage within snowy environments. These creatures possess large blue legs and tiny horns, and they exhibit rabbit-like hopping movements. While they can be defeated using any standard attack, Jak can prevent them from jumping over him by channeling red eco. Jak fought through a large number of these enemies during the "Survive the lurker-infested cave" mission.
Gol and Maia's citadel 3.png|Citadel bunnies atop their generators in the citadel.

They eventually evolved and became non-hostile, and were the species of the character Brutter and his tribe of lurker people, domesticated and enslaved by Haven citizens.

Babaks represent one of five known non-human sapient species in the Jak and Daxter series, accompanied by ottsels, Kor's species, Kaeden's species, and moncaws. However, only Brutter was ever shown to speak. They are also among lurker sharks and jungle fish as the only lurkers known to have survived the 300 years between The Precursor Legacy and Jak II.

short_desc: The ice lurker, also known as the ice monster, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy, spawning from the slippery ice regions of Snowy Mountain.
full_text: The ice lurker, also known as the ice monster, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy, spawning from the slippery ice regions of Snowy Mountain.
paragraphs: The ice lurker, also known as the ice monster, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy, spawning from the slippery ice regions of Snowy Mountain.

short_desc: The pedal-copter lurker, also known as a flying lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. Only found in Mountain Pass, they group in an entire scouting party, travelling to tell another lurker to detonate the mines situated in the Pass.
full_text:
The pedal-copter lurker, also known as a flying lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. Only found in Mountain Pass, they group in an entire scouting party, travelling to tell another lurker to detonate the mines situated in the Pass.

During the mission "Reach the end of Mountain Pass", Jak used Keira's A-Grav Zoomer to reach the end of the Pass before the lurkers do. If Jak and Daxter arrive at the end of the Mountain Pass in time, they will be able to crash into the lurker demolition worker just before he detonates the explosives.
paragraphs: The pedal-copter lurker, also known as a flying lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. Only found in Mountain Pass, they group in an entire scouting party, travelling to tell another lurker to detonate the mines situated in the Pass.

During the mission "Reach the end of Mountain Pass", Jak used Keira's A-Grav Zoomer to reach the end of the Pass before the lurkers do. If Jak and Daxter arrive at the end of the Mountain Pass in time, they will be able to crash into the lurker demolition worker just before he detonates the explosives.

short_desc: Klaww is a large lurker boss encountered in The Precursor Legacy. He operates from a volcanic section of the Mountain Pass, overlooking Rock Village. Klaww plays a significant role before the player confronts him, having terrorized Rock Village by bombarding it with boulders both prior to and during the Blue Sage's capture by Gol and Maia.
full_text: Klaww is a large lurker boss encountered in The Precursor Legacy. He operates from a volcanic section of the Mountain Pass, overlooking Rock Village. Klaww plays a significant role before the player confronts him, having terrorized Rock Village by bombarding it with boulders both prior to and during the Blue Sage's capture by Gol and Maia.

short_desc: The robber, also known as the flying lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They are lurkers found in the Precursor Basin near Rock Village. Robbers were responsible for scaring the rare lightning moles out of their underground holes. The moles' observer, the Geologist, gave Jak and Daxter the mission "Herd the moles into their hole".
full_text: The robber, also known as the flying lurker, is an enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They are lurkers found in the Precursor Basin near Rock Village. Robbers were responsible for scaring the rare lightning moles out of their underground holes. The moles' observer, the Geologist, gave Jak and Daxter the mission "Herd the moles into their hole".

Robbers were responsible for scaring the rare lightning moles out of their underground holes. The moles' observer, the Geologist, gave Jak and Daxter the mission "Herd the moles into their hole".

short_desc: The lightning mole is a medium-sized subterranean animal featured in The Precursor Legacy. They were featured in the mission "Herd the moles into their hole", during which Jak had to chase them back into the holes using the A-Grav Zoomer. They were reportedly scared out of their holes by a band of robbers in the Precursor Basin. They were initially discovered and subsequently researched by the Geologist, who rewarded Jak and Daxter a power cell for herding the moles back underground.

They were initially discovered and subsequently researched by the Geologist, who rewarded Jak and Daxter a power cell for herding the moles back underground.

short_desc: The lurker shark is a large orange shark-like creature that inhabits the oceans in The Precursor Legacy, serving mainly as the game's Invisible Wall mechanism to prevent the player from moving too far into the water. It is one of a few enemies in the Jak and Daxter series that is immune to Jak's attacks. The only way to avoid it is to stay on solid ground or in shallow water (including water connected to deep water where the depth is shallow due to reefs and inclines).
full_text: The lurker shark is a large orange shark-like creature that inhabits the oceans in The Precursor Legacy, serving mainly as the game's Invisible Wall mechanism to prevent the player from moving too far into the water. The only way to avoid it is to stay on solid ground or in shallow water (including water connected to deep water where the depth is shallow due to reefs and inclines).

short_desc: The green eco lurker, also known as the dark eco lurker, is a lurker enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They were created by Gol Acheron and Maia, being one of the only lurkers known for certain to be artificially created.
full_text:
The green eco lurker, also known as the dark eco lurker, is a lurker enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They were created by Gol Acheron and Maia, being one of the only lurkers known for certain to be artificially created.

These creatures were created when Gol and Maia's Precursor robot fired green eco into an open dark eco silo. They are large, spiky, lurker-like creatures, bearing the color of dark eco. They are very hostile, as they charge Jak to try to kill him.

They will charge and hit with their claws. Due to the small area in which they appear, there is little opportunity to run around. They are invulnerable to normal melee attacks, but they can be destroyed with yellow eco.
paragraphs: The green eco lurker, also known as the dark eco lurker, is a lurker enemy in The Precursor Legacy. They were created by Gol Acheron and Maia, being one of the only lurkers known for certain to be artificially created.

These creatures were created when Gol and Maia's Precursor robot fired green eco into an open dark eco silo. They are large, spiky, lurker-like creatures, bearing the color of dark eco. They are very hostile, as they charge Jak to try to kill him.

They will charge and hit with their claws. Due to the small area in which they appear, there is little opportunity to run around. They are invulnerable to normal melee attacks, but they can be destroyed with yellow eco.

short_desc: A Precursor robot is a type of Precursor statue used as a weapon by the Precursors, and seen throughout the Jak and Daxter series. Many of these massive structures could be seen in ruins, buried in rocky structures, although some were excavated and revived. Gol and Maia's Precursor robot was the first example of a fully functioning Precursor robot, however, it had undergone several alterations. The next, and final example of a functioning Precursor robot in whole was a Precursor robot controlled by Veger to attack Jak during Jak 3.
full_text:
A Precursor robot is a type of Precursor statue used as a weapon by the Precursors, and seen throughout the Jak and Daxter series. Many of these massive structures could be seen in ruins, buried in rocky structures, although some were excavated and revived. Gol and Maia's Precursor robot was the first example of a fully functioning Precursor robot, however, it had undergone several alterations. The next, and final example of a functioning Precursor robot in whole was a Precursor robot controlled by Veger to attack Jak during Jak 3.

Precursor robots are massive, humanoid-shaped robots, made entirely of Precursor metal, with an insectoid head and glowing, bug eyes, with an aardvark-like proboscis extending from the front of their face, very similar to the oracle. They have various cables and extensions connecting to various limbs and are able to defy gravity using an anti-gravity engine placed somewhere on the back of the device. They are extremely tough and very hard to defeat, as it took three explosive mine carts in order to defeat the Precursor robot seen in Jak 3.
